message:
docs(installation): separate community-maintained installer into its own subsection

Addresses the Copilot review comment (endorsed by @peatey in
https://github.com/opencost/opencost-website/pull/487#discussion_r3088794372):
the intro text frames the bullets as "supported ways of deploying OpenCost",
and placing the KubeStellar Console guided install in the same list made it
read as officially supported.

Pulls the KubeStellar Console entry out of the supported-ways list into a
dedicated "Community-maintained installers" subsection with explicit framing:
third-party, not officially supported, wraps existing install paths, and
direct users to Helm for any support or troubleshooting questions.

Preserves the formatting consistency fix from the previous commit (no inline
description on the bullet), since the subsection heading + prose now
communicate the community-maintained nature without re-introducing a per-bullet
description.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,6 +7,13 @@ There are several supported ways of deploying OpenCost, depending on your use ca
 * [Cloud service provider configuration](/docs/configuration)
 * [On-premises deployments](/docs/configuration/on-prem)
 * [Docker (no Kubernetes support)](/docs/installation/docker)
+
+For supported troubleshooting and the full set of configuration options, use [Helm](/docs/installation/helm).
+
+### Community-maintained installers
+
+These are third-party tools maintained by the community, not officially supported by the OpenCost project. They wrap one of the supported install paths above and are offered as a convenience for users who prefer a guided experience. For any support or troubleshooting questions, please use the official Helm path.
+
 * [Guided install with KubeStellar Console](/docs/installation/guided-install)
 
 ## Requirements
